description: Multidimensional taxonomy covering Deepfake Detection methodologies, Generation regimes, Impact and Intent.

entry.description: Content fully generated by AI without a prior real base. Source : Polidoro (2025) / Croitoru et al. (2024)
Modification of existing media via face-swap, lip-sync, or re-enactment. Source : Polidoro (2025)
Use of recent generative technologies leaving different signatures than GANs. Source : Croitoru et al. (2024)
Combined generation of audio, image, video, and text in a single synthetic production pipeline. Source : Croitoru et al. (2024)
Creation of synthetic speech or impersonation of a target's voice. Source : Gambín et al. (2024)
AI-generated text content. Source : Croitoru et al. (2024)
Targeted modification of age, gender, expression, or skin tone. Source : Khan, I. et al.
Generation technique using VAE architectures. Source : Khan, I. et al. (2025)
Examination of pixel-level anomalies, sensor noise, or compression artifacts. Source : Amerini et al. (2025)
Detection of motion inconsistencies or flickering between successive video frames. Source : Amerini et al. (2025)
Absence of eye blinking or heart rate anomalies detected via photoplethysmography. Source : Passos et al. (2024) / Çiftçi et al. (2024)
Visible boundary traces or inconsistent lighting reflections. Source : Sandotra et al. (2024)
Cross-modal detection exploiting desynchronisation between independently synthesised audio and visual streams. Source : Amerini et al. (2025)
Analysis of specific image features such as texture, color inconsistencies, and geometric face shape. Source : Sandotra et al. (2024)
Preprocessing step involving facial landmark detection and alignment for sequence analysis. Source : Khan, I. et al. (2025)
Evaluation of pixel and feature stability across successive video frames using RNN/LSTM. Source : Khan, I. et al. (2025)
Detection using deep convolutional neural networks to identify learned spatial inconsistencies. Source : Sandotra et al. (2024) / Khan, I. et al. (2025)
Identifying the specific AI model or camera fingerprint used. Source : MDPI Imaging (2025)
Deliberate intent to manipulate opinion or harm a target. Source : Polidoro (2025)
Humorous usage. Source : Polidoro (2025)
Use of synthetic imagery to destroy an individual's credibility. Source : Polidoro (2025) / Lee et al. (2023)
Demonstrations of AI capabilities for research purposes. Source : De Jong et al. (2025) / Polidoro (2025)
Use in cinema or arts. Source : Polidoro (2025)
Creating fake visual evidence to influence legal or scientific outcomes. Source : Oxford Academic (2024)
Production of fake academic evidence or hijacking of scientific expertise. Source : De Jong et al. (2025)
Misuse for profiling, emotion classification or ethno-phrenology. Source : Lee et al. (2023)
Intentional undermining of trust between citizens and institutions. Source : De Jong et al. (2025)
Targeting public figures or private citizens for impersonation. Source : Polidoro (2025) / Sandotra et al. (2025)
Saturation of the information space with automated textual content. Source : Croitoru et al. (2024)
Production of synthetic sexually explicit or degrading content featuring private individuals without consent.
